summary:
The provided evidence does not include data on central line-associated bloodstream infections (CLABSIs), nor do they support claims of sustainably maintained zero infection rates. Due to well-established risks of bacterial colonization and transmission through invasive vascular access, clinically realistic outcomes suggest that a zero CLABSI rate is unattainable over time.  

Note: This summary excludes all non-clinical content and stays focused only on direct relevance to infection rates and practicality within healthcare settings.
